1.gl: properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. to be eminent (as a superintendent, especially of the Temple services and its music); to be permanent
1.kj: excel, chief musician (singer), oversee(-r), set forward.
1.dv: a primitive root;  also as denominative from H5331 (נֶצַח)

1.gl: David, the youngest son of Jesse
1.dv: rarely (fully); דָּוִיד; from the same as H1730 (דּוֹד); loving;

1.gl: properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes

2.gl: to give, used with greatest latitude of application (put, make, etc.)
2.kj: add, apply, appoint, ascribe, assign, [idiom] avenge, [idiom] be (healed), bestow, bring (forth, hither), cast, cause, charge, come, commit, consider, count, [phrase] cry, deliver

2.kj: how (long, oft, (-soever)), (no-) thing, what (end, good, purpose, thing), whereby(-fore, -in, -to, -with), (for) why.

2.gl: an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character

2.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)

3.gl: strength in various applications (force, security, majesty, praise)
